operation: First, initialize a workplane on the XZ coordinate plane, sketch a regular 6-sided polygon with a diameter of 80 units, then extrude this sketch 150 units along the positive Y axis to form a solid hexagonal prism. Next, select the minimum-Y end face of the prism and perform an inward shell operation with a thickness of 6 units, hollowing the prism into an open-ended hexagonal tube with 6 unit thick walls. Then select the inner wire of the minimum-Y face, translate it 1 unit in the negative Y direction, apply a 2D inward offset of 1 unit to the wire, extrude this adjusted profile 150 units along the Y axis as a separate non-combined solid. Finally, select the maximum Z face and the second-highest Z face of the two solids, and perform an inward shell operation with 3 unit thickness to create the open top of the structure.
geometry: This is an elongated double-walled open-top trough with a regular hexagonal cross-section aligned along its longitudinal axis. The inner and outer hexagonal wall layers are concentric with a small uniform gap between them, both featuring consistent wall thickness. The upper section of the hexagonal profile is removed to form a wide open cavity that runs the full length of the trough, which is open at both of its longitudinal ends.
function: This is a rigid double-walled hexagonal trough built for material handling use cases, including bulk material feeder channels, storage trays, or liquid containment gutters. Its double-wall structure provides enhanced structural strength and thermal insulation, while the open top and open ends support convenient material loading and continuous material flow along its length.
